findings.rationale: The image has aria-hidden="true" but also carries a descriptive alt text ("Logo von All Reis Geschäftskunde"). This is a coding inconsistency: aria-hidden="true" removes the element from the accessibility tree, making the alt text inaccessible to assistive technology. The presence of meaningful alt text alongside aria-hidden="true" suggests informative intent that is being suppressed, violating SC 1.1.1. Either the aria-hidden should be removed (if the logo is informative) or the alt text should be empty (if it is truly decorative).

findings.rationale: The link has a clear and descriptive accessible name 'Kontakt & Standorte' (Contact & Locations) that explicitly states its purpose. The href '/kontakt' confirms this is a navigation link to the contact and locations page. The purpose is unambiguous from the accessible name alone, requiring no reliance on surrounding context.

findings.rationale: The link text 'Zum Sortiment' (meaning 'To the Product Range' in German) clearly indicates the link's purpose—to navigate to the product/assortment section of the Asia Food Store website. The accessible name is sufficiently descriptive on its own, and the surrounding context about fresh herbs, vegetables, and Asian food products reinforces this purpose.

findings.rationale: The link text 'Produkte ansehen' (View Products) clearly indicates the action of viewing/browsing products. This is reinforced by the surrounding context which discusses 'Unser Sortiment' (Our Assortment) and explicitly invites users to view the product selection. The href '/sortiment' further confirms the destination is the product catalog.

The aria-haspopup='dialog' attribute further reinforces this function. The accessible name alone is sufficient to understand the link's purpose.

findings.rationale: The accessible name 'Mehr über uns' (More about us) clearly indicates the link's purpose: to navigate to more information about the company. This is reinforced by the surrounding context which introduces 'Wir sind Broth3rs!' and describes the family business, making it clear that clicking this link will provide additional details about the company. The URL '/ueber-uns' (about-us) further supports this interpretation.

findings.rationale: The link has an empty accessible name (no alt text on the image, no aria-label, no text content) and relies solely on a logo image to convey purpose. While the surrounding context and ARIA snapshot indicate this is a 'home' link, the accessible name itself is completely empty, making the link purpose inaccessible to screen reader users who cannot see the image.

findings.rationale: The link has an empty accessible name and contains only an SVG icon with no text alternative. While the href reveals it links to Instagram, the link itself provides no accessible purpose—users relying on assistive technology cannot determine that this is a social media link without relying solely on the URL structure.

findings.rationale: The link has an empty accessible name and contains only an SVG icon (Facebook logo). While the href reveals it links to a Facebook page, the link itself provides no accessible text or label that would allow users to understand its purpose without relying on visual recognition of the icon or URL inspection.

findings.rationale: The link has an empty accessible name and contains only an SVG icon with no text alternative. While the href reveals it links to a TikTok profile (@broth3rsasiafood), users relying on assistive technology cannot determine the link's purpose from the accessible name alone, and the surrounding context (footer address and store information) does not clarify that this is a social media link.
